address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

msContents.summary: A collection of some 59 short prayers by the Báb, belonging to what Browne calls the "second grade" or "style" of the Bayán (see F. 15 (BBF. 2) for Browne's fuller explanation of the "five grades"). Owing to their number Browne gives only the opening words of each; the manuscript ends with a colophon dated "the second day of the first month of the year 30" (a Bábí/Badí' calendar date).

msItem.title: Min āthār al-bayān
من آثار البيان
Prayer from the Arabic Bayān
msItem.incipit: هو العطوف الرحيم الرؤوف الكريم... (no. 1, f. 2b)
msItem.explicit: شهد الله انه لا اله الا هو العزيز المحبوب... (no. 59(?))
msItem.colophon: colophon f. 236b dated "the second day of the first month of the year 30" (a Bábí/Badí' calendar date)
msItem.note: Sample further incipits, per Browne: no. 9 (f. 63b) "بسم الله الامنع الاقدس سبحانی اللهم يا الهی..."; no. 29 (f. 114a) "بسم الله الامنع الاقدس و اذا كنت ليلة الجمعة..." (cert="low", resp="#ocr-draft" throughout).

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBF. 1 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

handDesc.handNote: Written in a peculiar ta'líq much used by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel for the transcription of the sacred books.

history.origin: Place and date of transcription not stated by Browne beyond the colophon date noted above.
history.provenance: Accompanied the first letter Browne received from Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel (despatched 29 July, received 15 August 1889), in which Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel explained that most of his books and epistles had been plundered by "certain persons," and that a book of twenty folios was all that remained available to send at that time (quoted by Browne in Persian with his own English translation). Forwarded from Famagusta, Cyprus.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: "Manuscript description based on. R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932. ", abbreviated by the OCIMCO project team; provenance expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábí Manuscripts," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Jul. 1892, pp. 451-462.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBF_1.xml) into this Fihrist record: summary, item notes, incipit/explicit samples, physical description, hand note, and provenance history rewritten from Browne's 1892 JRAS catalogue account (pp. 451-462). Arabic quotations are unproofed OCR (Tesseract), flagged cert="low". Person/place authority keys reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne, Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel, Cyprus). Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
Andrew Morrison Removed origDate tags around something which is not a date using fix-dates.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
